figures.caption: Fig. 1: We contribute a principled inference-time guidance framework for streaming generative policies. It enables fast reactive behavior within the action chunk, and can be deployed in either a training-based or training-free manner.

Fig. 2: Safety-Reward trade-off. SSIP consistently achieves more favorable trade-offs compared to the baselines, with its Pareto frontiers closer to the top-right corner of the reward–safety plane. Among the SSIP variants, CCG-P dominates across the evaluated range, while STEG outperforms the CCG-D variant.

Fig. 3: SSIP with CCG-P guidance in static and dynamic obstacle tasks. Red arrows (→) represent the base policy while blue arrows (→) represent guidance. We can observe the guidance acts to push the end-effector away from the obstacles.

Fig. 6: Comprehensive Safety-Reward trade-off (Average Reward vs. Safety Rate) across various baselines. We primarily report ODE variants in the main text, as the stochastic noise introduced by SDE inference was found to degrade performance.
TABLE V: Hyperparameters for Policy Training
